saturation.visual_no_code_automation: HIGH — Zapier, Make, n8n dominate. Red ocean.
saturation.open_source_self_host_infra: MEDIUM — niche but growing. Clear demand for POSIX/edge.
saturation.multi_agent_swarm_cloud: HIGH and growing — but mostly cloud-first. Edge angle is differentiator.
saturation.phone_based_ai_compute: VERY LOW — near-zero competition. First-mover possible.
saturation.ai_saas_factory_automation: MEDIUM-HIGH — early movers (AutoGPT, Devika) lost steam. Agentic 2.0 rising.
saturation.free_tier_ai_orchestration: LOW — most tools lock free tier. UOM $0-cost policy is differentiator.

tier_0_free.label: Free Tier (current default)
tier_0_free.suitable_for: solo dev, prototyping, demo, early beta
tier_0_free.risk: Rate limits hit under load. No SLA. Model availability varies.
tier_0_free.source: ESTIMATE: OpenRouter free tier observed limits

tier_1_startup.label: Startup Tier (first paying customers)
tier_1_startup.risk: Cost spikes on long QA loops. Need hard budget caps.

tier_2_growth.label: Growth Tier (scaling)
tier_2_growth.risk: Tight margin if price-to-cost ratio not calibrated.

tier_3_enterprise.label: Enterprise Tier
tier_3_enterprise.risk: Requires significant capital before revenue at this scale.

phase_0_pre_launch.label: Pre-Launch (now → 3 months)
phase_0_pre_launch.key_milestone: Working UOM mesh stable, omni-saas Layer 3 hooks complete

phase_1_early_beta.label: Early Beta (3-6 months)
revenue_sources: Ko-fi / Buy Me a Coffee one-time (₹200-500/transaction)
phase_1_early_beta.key_milestone: First public demo. First sponsor. README + docs polished.

phase_2_first_customers.label: First Paying Customers (6-12 months)
revenue_sources: Managed UOM setup service: ₹2000-5000 one-time per customer
phase_2_first_customers.key_milestone: omni-saas S0+S1 working. First customer paying for swarm output.
phase_2_first_customers.source: ESTIMATE: comparable solo SaaS on IndieHackers at 12 months median

phase_3_growth.label: Growth Phase (12-24 months)
revenue_sources: API reseller margin on model calls: ₹2000-10000/month
phase_3_growth.key_milestone: Full omni-saas S0-S4 pipeline. Product-market fit signal.
phase_3_growth.source: ESTIMATE: top 15th percentile solo SaaS at 18 months

phase_4_scale.label: Scale Phase (24-36 months)
revenue_sources: White-label UOM mesh for other solo devs / micro-agencies
Swarm output as a service (research packs, build packs)
Revenue share on Stripe products built by swarms
phase_4_scale.key_milestone: Requires hire, or strong automation reducing manual ops to <4hr/week.
phase_4_scale.source: ESTIMATE: top 5th percentile solo SaaS 30 months

scenario_conservative.label: Conservative (most likely)
scenario_conservative.note: Only achievable if first 5 paying customers reached at month 6

scenario_realistic.label: Realistic (median path)
scenario_realistic.note: Requires consistent shipping + content presence (Shadow Narratives cross-promo helps)

scenario_optimistic.label: Optimistic (top 10% scenario)
scenario_optimistic.note: Requires enterprise contract or viral product launch

roi_summary.warning: Solo non-developer SaaS median MRR at 12 months is under $500/month globally. INR pricing gives competitive edge in Indian market. Singapore re-entry may unlock higher-ticket consulting contracts.

current_inventory.laptop: HP Pavilion 15-n010tx — i3-3217U, 4GB RAM, HDD (failing SATA)
current_inventory.phone1: Xiaomi Mi 8 (dipper, SD845, SDK 35) — secondary QEMU host
blockers: 4GB RAM limits QEMU guest performance and parallel workloads
Failing SATA cable causes I/O instability
i3-3217U (2012 CPU) bottlenecks any ML-adjacent workloads
No NVMe on current laptop — all storage on spinning disk

investments.reason: Eliminates I/O failures that corrupt QEMU disk images

investments.item: 16GB DDR3L SO-DIMM RAM upgrade (HP Pavilion compatible)
investments.reason: Doubles QEMU capacity. Enables 2x guests + Zen Loop + verifier simultaneously
investments.where: OLX / Amazon India / local PC shop
investments.note: HP Pavilion 15-n010tx supports max 8GB per slot, 2 slots = 16GB

investments.reason: 10x I/O improvement. QEMU disk images on SSD = dramatically faster guest boot
investments.where: Amazon India — brands: WD Green, Kingston A400

phase_1_immediate_under_5000_inr.expected_uplift: 50-100% QEMU performance improvement. Eliminates data corruption risk.

investments.reason: Replaces phones as reliable background worker. ARM64 native. 24/7 uptime. No battery drain.
investments.note: Better alternative to Phone1 for QEMU aarch64 workloads

investments.item: 4TB External HDD (USB 3.0) for artifact + bundle storage
investments.reason: Git bundle archive, QCOW2 image backups, research cache persistence
Wired LAN eliminates WiFi instability for Phone2 hotspot setup
Offload Git remote, webhook receiver, and light API relay away from phones

phase_2_beta_launch_5000_30000_inr.expected_uplift: Stable 24/7 mesh without phone battery dependency. Customer-grade uptime.

investments.item: Mini PC / NUC (Intel N100, 16GB RAM, 512GB NVMe)
investments.reason: Replace HP Pavilion as primary. N100 = 4x performance, fanless, 8W TDP. NVMe fast.
investments.where: Amazon India — Beelink Mini S12 Pro or MINISFORUM UN100
investments.note: This becomes the permanent coordinator. HP Pavilion → secondary test node.

investments.item: Upgraded VPS (4 vCPU, 8GB RAM) for swarm factory staging
investments.reason: Run Swarm B (Build) and Swarm C (QA) on cloud without touching home hardware
investments.where: Hetzner CPX21 ~€8/month, or Oracle Cloud Always Free (2 ARM64 VMs)

investments.reason: Tamil Nadu power cuts kill QEMU disk integrity. UPS = 20-min ride-out.

phase_3_first_revenue_30000_100000_inr.expected_uplift: Production-grade mesh. First customer SLA-able. Swarm factory can run full 2-4 week QA.

investments.item: Dedicated server (Hetzner AX41-NVMe) — 6-core Ryzen, 64GB RAM, 2x512GB NVMe
investments.reason: Host multiple customer meshes and parallel swarm pipelines concurrently

investments.item: AI accelerator card (RX 6600 or RTX 3060) if local LLM needed
investments.reason: Only needed if paid API costs exceed ₹30K/month. Keep free-tier policy as long as possible.

phase_4_scale_100000_plus_inr.note: Phase 4 hardware only unlocks if Phase 3 revenue targets are hit. No speculative purchase.

critical_pitfalls.title: The Hardware Cliff
critical_pitfalls.description: HP Pavilion 4GB RAM + failing SATA = production environment that corrupts its own disk. Every QEMU guest crash risks the QA artifacts and bundle state. The SATA cable is the #1 risk item. Fix it before any customer relies on this mesh.
critical_pitfalls.mitigation: SATA cable replacement (₹200) is the single highest-ROI action in the entire roadmap.

critical_pitfalls.title: Free Model Availability Is Not Guaranteed
critical_pitfalls.description: OpenRouter free tiers change without notice. deepseek-v4-flash-free, big-pickle, and other free models may impose rate limits, disappear, or degrade. The entire Zen Loop depends on $0-cost models. Any change breaks the pipeline.
critical_pitfalls.mitigation: Maintain a model fallback pool of 6+ free models. Implement cost-sentinel: abort if model shows any non-zero pricing. Keep paid-model code path dormant but ready.

critical_pitfalls.title: Solo Non-Developer Bottleneck
critical_pitfalls.description: UOM is AI-generated code managed by a prompt engineer. This is a strength (speed) and a risk (depth). When a bug requires deep C/kernel/assembly knowledge, or a customer asks for a feature outside LLM comfort zone, the pipeline stalls. Time-to-fix can be 3-10x longer than a developer.
critical_pitfalls.mitigation: Define explicit scope boundaries in customer contracts. Stick to POSIX sh / Python / Markdown layer. Outsource kernel-level work to community or defer.

critical_pitfalls.title: Phone Dependency Is Not Customer-Grade
critical_pitfalls.description: Phone2 as a hotspot + QEMU host + tunnel relay is a single point of failure. If the battery swells, the phone breaks, or Android kills background processes, the entire mesh loses its gateway. No customer SLA can be built on this.
critical_pitfalls.mitigation: Phase 2 hardware investment (RPi 5 + VPS) removes phone dependency from critical path. Do not sign SLA before Phase 2 hardware is online.

critical_pitfalls.title: Market Saturation in Visual AI Automation
critical_pitfalls.description: The no-code automation market (Zapier, Make, n8n) is saturated and well-funded. Competing directly on features is a losing bet. UOM's edge is POSIX/edge/privacy-first — but that niche is smaller.
critical_pitfalls.mitigation: Position UOM as the back-end mesh for builders, not a UI tool for non-technicals. Target: developers, homelab power users, solo founders who want control without cloud lock-in.

critical_pitfalls.title: Multi-Week QA Factory Is Not Free
critical_pitfalls.description: The multi-swarm SaaS pipeline (Swarm A-D) requires weeks of QA. On free models with rate limits, a 2-week QA soak on free tier will exhaust daily limits multiple times. The QA phase in practice will take 2-4x longer than on paid models.
critical_pitfalls.mitigation: Implement a budget-gated paid-model mode for Swarm C (QA) only. $10-30 for a 2-week QA run is acceptable. This is the one place where free-tier policy can be selectively relaxed.

critical_pitfalls.title: Singapore Work Permit + Income Declaration
critical_pitfalls.description: If Singapore S-Pass is approved and Dharani returns to employment, running a revenue-generating SaaS simultaneously may conflict with employment contract or MOM regulations on secondary income.
critical_pitfalls.mitigation: Review employment contract for IP and secondary income clauses before monetizing. Keep UOM as open-source with Patreon/sponsorship model (generally allowed) vs. active SaaS contracts (may need employer clearance).

critical_pitfalls.title: agentic AI Race Commoditizes the Stack
critical_pitfalls.description: OpenAI, Google, Anthropic, and Microsoft are all building native agent orchestration. By 2027, much of what UOM manually wires together (model routing, tool use, multi-step pipelines) may be 1-click in the major platforms.
critical_pitfalls.mitigation: UOM's moat is the EDGE + POSIX + zero-cost + phone mesh layer — not the LLM routing logic. The platform will commoditize; the hardware autonomy will not. Lean into the edge story.

critical_pitfalls.title: Revenue Timeline Is Longer Than Expected
critical_pitfalls.description: Solo non-developer SaaS median time to $500 MRR globally is 14-22 months. In India, INR pricing gives competitive pricing power but a smaller absolute TAM. First paying customer typically takes 6-10 months from stable MVP.
critical_pitfalls.mitigation: Start monetizing earlier with low-risk revenue: GitHub Sponsors, consulting time, Shadow Narratives cross-promo. Do not depend on swarm factory revenue in the first 9 months.

critical_pitfalls.title: cinai / AGI Disruption Risk
critical_pitfalls.description: If cinai-level autonomous AI emerges within 18-24 months, the role of a human prompt-engineer orchestrating free models becomes redundant at the infrastructure level. The value shifts to: who owns the deployment stack and customer relationships.
critical_pitfalls.mitigation: Build customer lock-in through data portability and on-premise/edge value, not model quality. When AGI orchestrates itself, the edge mesh (UOM) and the customer trust layer become MORE valuable, not less.

honest_expectations.year_1_most_likely_outcome: 0-1 paying customers. ₹0-10K MRR. Strong open-source cred. Well-documented mesh. Active GitHub Sponsors funnel started.
honest_expectations.year_2_most_likely_outcome: 5-25 paying customers. ₹10K-50K MRR. omni-saas factory in beta. First consulting revenue from Singapore or Indian market.
what_will_actually_kill_this: Abandoning the project after 6 months of no revenue
Trying to compete with n8n or Zapier on their terms
Ignoring hardware stability (SATA cable) for feature building
Running out of free model access with no paid fallback plan
Singapore employment conflicting with SaaS income
what_gives_this_the_best_chance: Shipping a working demo and posting on IndieHackers, HackerNews Show HN
Shadow Narratives YouTube cross-promo as distribution channel
Niche positioning: POSIX AI mesh for homelab builders
Singapore network providing first enterprise consulting contracts
Keeping costs at $0 until revenue justifies paid models
